---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_macro_panel.csv")

  # Variables
  row_var: "district"        # categorical axis -> rows
  col_var: "year"            # categorical axis -> columns
  value_var: "literacy_rate" # cell value (ignored when aggregate = "count")

  # How to get one value per (row, col) cell:
  #   "none"  = data already has one row per cell; use value_var directly
  #   "count" = count rows per cell
  #   "mean" / "sum" = aggregate value_var per cell
  aggregate: "none"

  # Print the numeric value inside each cell
  show_values: true

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Literacy Rate (%)"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Category Matrix: Heatmap"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 5.5,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed)
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)

# value_var is only required when it will actually be used
required <- c(params$row_var, params$col_var)
if (params$aggregate %in% c("none", "mean", "sum")) {
  required <- c(required, params$value_var)
}
cerp_validate(raw_data, required)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
base <- raw_data %>%
  drop_na(all_of(c(params$row_var, params$col_var))) %>%
  mutate(RowCat = as.character(.data[[params$row_var]]),
         ColCat = as.character(.data[[params$col_var]]))

# Collapse to exactly one value per (row, col) cell
cell <- switch(params$aggregate,
  "count" = base %>% count(RowCat, ColCat, name = "Value"),
  "mean"  = base %>%
    mutate(V = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)) %>%
    group_by(RowCat, ColCat) %>% summarize(Value = mean(V, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop"),
  "sum"   = base %>%
    mutate(V = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)) %>%
    group_by(RowCat, ColCat) %>% summarize(Value = sum(V, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop"),
  "none"  = {
    if (anyDuplicated(base[c("RowCat", "ColCat")]) > 0) stop(
      "Multiple rows share the same (", params$row_var, ", ", params$col_var,
      ") cell, but aggregate is 'none'. Set 'aggregate' to 'count', 'mean', ",
      "or 'sum' so the template knows how to combine them.", call. = FALSE)
    base %>% transmute(RowCat, ColCat,
                       Value = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var))
  },
  stop("Unknown aggregate option: '", params$aggregate,
       "'. Use 'none', 'count', 'mean', or 'sum'.", call. = FALSE)
)

# Fill the full grid so empty cells appear (grey) rather than vanish
plot_data <- cell %>% complete(RowCat, ColCat)

# Order columns numerically when they are numbers (e.g. years), else keep as-is;
# order rows by their mean value so the strongest categories sit together.
col_levels <- unique(plot_data$ColCat)
if (!any(is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(col_levels))))) {
  col_levels <- col_levels[order(as.numeric(col_levels))]
}
row_order <- plot_data %>% group_by(RowCat) %>%
  summarize(m = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>% arrange(m) %>% pull(RowCat)

plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(ColCat = factor(ColCat, levels = col_levels),
         RowCat = factor(RowCat, levels = row_order))

n_rows <- nlevels(plot_data$RowCat)
val_digits <- if (params$aggregate == "mean") 1 else 0
mid_val <- mean(range(plot_data$Value, na.rm = TRUE))
```


```{r plot-heatmap-matrix, fig.height=max(4, 0.55 * n_rows + 2.5)}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = ColCat, y = RowCat, fill = Value)) +

  geom_tile(color = cerp_cols[["bg"]], linewidth = 0.8) +

  # Sequential ramp: pale box tint (low) -> steel blue (high); empty cells grey
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = unname(cerp_cols["box"]),
    high = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]),
    na.value = "#e6e2d9",
    name = params$metric_name
  ) +

  theme_cerp() +

  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "left",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.8, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.title = element_text(family = cerp_font_caption, size = 10,
                                color = cerp_cols[["subtle"]]),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = cerp_cols[["text"]])
  ) +

  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else paste("Category Matrix:", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else "Each cell is shaded by value; darker means higher. Grey cells have no data.",
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else str_to_title(params$col_var),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else str_to_title(params$row_var),
    caption = params$source_note
  )

# Optional in-cell value labels, with contrast-aware text color
if (isTRUE(params$show_values)) {
  p <- p + geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(is.na(Value), "",
                       formatC(Value, format = "f", digits = val_digits)),
        color = Value > mid_val),
    family = cerp_font_caption, size = 3.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = cerp_cols[["text"]], "TRUE" = "#FFFFFF"),
                       na.value = cerp_cols[["subtle"]])
}

p
```
